title: pittosporum tobira f. macrophylla
intro: Pittosporum tobira f. macrophylla, also known as the Japanese mock orange, is a variety of the Pittosporum tobira plant, a flowering evergreen shrub native to Japan and other parts of Asia. The leaves and stems of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Pittosporum tobira f. macrophylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Pittosporum tobira f. macrophylla can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for use in treating various health conditions.
